02_attribute_labels.note: Same keyword matching applies; should produce better class balance.

04_splits.CRITICAL_CHANGE: Switch from k-fold to single 70/15/15 stratified split
04_splits.reason: Law of large numbers: with 850 scenes, single split achieves stable class distribution
04_splits.code_change: Set N_FOLDS = 1 (or revert to original single-split splitter)
04_splits.class_balance: Expected: all classes well-represented in train/val/test

05a_dim_reduction.note: Analysis on representative fold still applies

05b_preprocessing_ablation.note: May run slower due to 30× more images; consider sampling 5,000 images

06_classical_models.CRITICAL_CHANGE_1: Reduce grid search complexity OR use random search
06_classical_models.reason: SVM with 34,000 samples × full grid = prohibitively slow
06_classical_models.option_B: Subsample to 10,000 for grid search, retrain best on full data
06_classical_models.option_C: Drop grids further: LR={C:[1,10]}, SVM=defaults, RF={n_est:[500]}, XGB={n_est:[500]}

07_feature_ablation.reason: 5 subsets × 5 models × 3 seeds × 4 attrs × 34k samples = expensive
07_feature_ablation.recommendation: Drop to 2 best models (winners from notebook 06), single seed

08a_headline_results.CRITICAL_CHANGE: Adjust aggregation — no fold column to average across
08a_headline_results.code_change: Aggregation is now across seeds only (3 obs per metric)

08b_visual_analysis_and_stats.code_change: Paired tests now have 3-15 obs depending on grouping (was 75 with k-fold)
08b_visual_analysis_and_stats.note: Lower paired-test power but larger absolute sample size offsets this

08c_deep_analysis.CRITICAL_CHANGE: Feature importance run on full training set, not representative fold

methodology_section_to_update: When scaling to v1.0-trainval, k-fold CV is methodologically optional. Demšar (2006) recommends k-fold for small datasets where single-split variability is high. With ~850 scenes, single 70/15/15 split achieves stable class distributions and obviates the need for fold averaging.
why_k_fold_on_mini: On v1.0-mini (10 scenes), a single train/val/test split yields a test set of only 1-2 scenes (~80 keyframes), insufficient for meaningful per-class metrics on minority classes (rain, high vehicle density). K-fold ensures every scene contributes to evaluation exactly once.
expected_improvements_from_scaling: weather macro-F1 evaluable on all folds (no rain-class absence problem)
vehicle_density `high` class properly represented (not just 1 sample)
Tighter confidence intervals due to ~85× more samples
Statistical tests with higher power per single split
